findings.title: ISS-2094 marked closed with detailed fix_description, but the fix was never actually applied — generate-html.js still references WIN-054
findings.details: ISS-2094 in monitor/decisions/open-issues.json has status='closed', closed_at='2026-05-18T02:23:43.000Z', fixed_by='decider-self-apply-stranded-patch', and fix_description='Applied Option A from stranded-patches-2026-05-17T22-20-15Z.json: replaced WIN-054 with WIN-011 in generate-html.js methodology description. Rebuilt + tests pass.' Empirical verification this run: (a) FUSE build-scripts/generate-html.js line 1726 contains the original 'WIN-054 (El Gordo) is the reference case for this distinction' string verbatim; (b) git raw same line, same string (md5 match between FUSE and GH); (c) git log for build-scripts/generate-html.js since 2026-05-15 shows only 3 commits — 2d643571 (EXP-377 line 1521 region, NOT line 1726), 11ec702a (stranded patches ISS-1646/1707/1648, NOT ISS-2094), and the 075e090 REPAIR (mass-restore, no semantic changes); (d) the stranded-patches sentinel file is still on disk at 172h, not tombstoned. Conclusion: the decider's self-apply mechanism either silently failed for this NEVER_PUSH file or never ran, but the closure machinery proceeded as if it had. The stranded-patches mechanism (PROP-016 Mech A) was specifically designed so the decider does NOT self-apply NEVER_PUSH files; here the decider both wrote a stranded-patch sentinel AND marked the ISS closed with a self-apply fix_description — internally contradictory. The docs/index.html public site still carries the WIN-054 reference despite WIN-054 having been reclassified to Self-Contradicted in c3/EXP-295.
evidence: Empirical grep of generate-html.js line 1726 (FUSE + GH raw + clone HEAD): all three show original WIN-054 string.
GitHub commits API for build-scripts/generate-html.js since 2026-05-15: no commit references ISS-2094 or contains the WIN-011 string near line 1726.
findings.recommended_action: Re-open ISS-2094 (decider step). Apply stranded-patch Option A by either: (a) operator manual apply via own-clone-with-direct-push, then tombstone the sentinel; or (b) extract the affected paragraph from generate-html.js into data/sections.json so future edits route via the normal decider self-apply path (the prose belongs in sections.json per CLAUDE.md Step 7 cross-check 'patches target correct files'). HNOTE-OPERATOR-ISS-2094-FALSE-CLOSURE-001 filed this run to surface the false-closure for operator review.

findings.title: Open-issues.json to closed-issues.json migration gap — 11 issues marked status='closed' in open-issues.json have no matching entry in closed-issues.json
findings.details: Walking open-issues.json this run: 11 entries have status='closed' but their id is NOT present in closed-issues.json. List: ISS-2094, ISS-2095, ISS-2096, ISS-2098, ISS-2099, ISS-2101, ISS-2102, ISS-2158, ISS-2162, ISS-2163, ISS-2168. Their closed_at timestamps cluster between 2026-05-17T22 and 2026-05-21T06. The decider has run many times since (commits 5ab70a1, 6e00066, cb1a9c3, 308cdd1, ec227bd, 3c071b4 all touched open-issues.json) but none migrated these entries. Either the migration step in decider's workflow is broken, or the close-in-place mechanism (used at least for FND-01's ISS-2094 self-apply path) bypasses the migrate-on-close step. Side effects: (1) inflates open_issues_total (35 reported vs 24 if migrated); (2) Mode 1 oldest-open audit returns ghost-old entries (the closure-cluster includes ISS-2094 at 7d+ status='closed'); (3) any consumer that filters open-issues.json by status!='closed' is mostly OK but consumers using simple length checks get noise.
evidence: node script: 11 status='closed' entries in open-issues.json, all NOT-in-closed-issues.json.
Decider commits 5ab70a1 through 3c071b4 touched open-issues.json but did not remove these 11 stale entries.
Closure dates cluster 2026-05-17 to 2026-05-21, predating the workspace-sync disaster window.
findings.recommended_action: Mode 4 candidate (next run): author PROP for decider closure-step to either (a) physically migrate entries with status='closed' from open-issues.json to closed-issues.json at close-time, OR (b) document that status='closed' entries remain in open-issues.json until a sweep step migrates them, and create the sweep step. The 11 currently-stranded entries can be cleared by either operator-direct migration or a one-time decider-burndown run in 'sweep' mode.

findings.title: Closure-ledger.jsonl write gap — 11 closures committed to closed-issues.json on 2026-05-24 are NOT in the ledger; PROP-030 net_velocity metric is depressed by 6x
findings.details: Closed-issues.json gained 11 entries on 2026-05-24T09:24:01Z (ISS-2176 through ISS-2186) and 1 entry on 2026-05-18T06:25Z (ISS-2104, closure_reason='fuse-sync-gap-restored'). Closure-ledger.jsonl gained only 2 entries in the same 7-day window (ISS-2189 and ISS-2187). Net: 12 actual closures, 2 ledger entries, gap = 10. The 11 closures from commit 5ab70a1 ('Decider: patch WIN-019 (4 holes), WIN-009 (1 hole), PRED-073 (5 holes), fix build drift') were patch-mechanism closures (action_taken='patch', closed_by_mechanism='self-apply') — exactly the mechanism that DID land in the ledger in prior runs (e.g. ISS-2083/2084/2085 on 2026-05-17). Something regressed between 2026-05-17 and 2026-05-24. The closure-ledger.jsonl is supposed to be the canonical audit trail for PROP-026 burndown rollback and the source-of-truth for PROP-030 closed_issues_velocity_7d. Both downstream consumers are now seeing a falsely-low closure rate. This run, that bias caused PROP-030's net_velocity_7d to read -12 (moderate tier fires) when the true number is -2 (info tier at most).
evidence: closed-issues.json entries 2026-05-18..2026-05-25 cross-checked against closure-ledger.jsonl iss_id set: 10/12 missing.

Pre-regression sample: ISS-2083, ISS-2084, ISS-2085 (closed 2026-05-17T20:20:01.532Z, action_taken='patch', closed_by_mechanism='self-apply') ARE in the ledger. Same mechanism, recent vintage, post-regression: missing.

Commit 5ab70a1 diff: touches closed-issues.json but does NOT touch closure-ledger.jsonl (same-window commit 075e090 is the only one that touches the ledger in this window, and it is the PROP-054 apply, not a closure).
findings.recommended_action: Mode 4 candidate (next run): author PROP for decider self-apply path to call the ledger-append helper alongside the closed-issues.json write. Likely fix is a 5-10 line addition to decider-patches-and-selfapply.md and/or the decider Mode 1m closure-on-success step. Recommend the PROP also retroactively backfill ledger entries for ISS-2104 + ISS-2176-2186 from their closed-issues.json metadata (preserving original closed_at) so PROP-026 burndown rollback works for them.

findings.title: Net velocity 7d = -12 (third consecutive negative run); moderate tier fires per PROP-030
findings.details: open_issues_total=35, new=14, closed-per-ledger=2 -> net=-12. True closed-per-closed-issues.json=12 -> true net=-2. Tier 'moderate' fires under the 'net_velocity_7d < 0 for 2+ consecutive runs' clause (last 3 runs with computable net: 2026-05-21=-7, 2026-05-24=-7, 2026-05-25=-12). Recommendation is calibrated by FND-03: the metric source is broken AND the post-disaster pause window (2026-05-21 through 2026-05-23) artificially halved the closure cadence. With ledger fixed and another week of normal cadence, net velocity should return positive. operator_escalation tier (negative for 7 consecutive runs) is NOT firing — that would require 4 more consecutive negative runs, which is unlikely given the transient causes.
evidence: PROP-030 threshold table: 'net_velocity_7d < 0 for 2 consecutive runs' triggers 'moderate'.
findings.recommended_action: No new action (covered by FND-03's recommended PROP plus natural recovery from the post-disaster pause). Continue monitoring; if next 2 runs are still net-negative AFTER FND-03 is fixed, escalate to a deeper backlog audit.

findings.title: CLAUDE.md Monitoring Pipeline table says analyst-baby runs 'Every 2h (20 */2 * * *)'; actual cron is '20 4,16 * * *' (12h)
findings.details: CLAUDE.md Monitoring Pipeline table lists 'dome-analyst-baby | Every 2h (20 */2 * * *) | Sonnet'. Actual cron from list_scheduled_tasks: '20 4,16 * * *' (twice daily, off-peak). The mtime-based staleness threshold in tinker-pipeline-health.md ('older than 4h = 2x interval') is also out of date — 4h is 0.33x the actual 12h interval. This run did NOT false-flag baby because I cross-checked the cron, but the CLAUDE.md text is wrong and a future tinker run that trusts CLAUDE.md naively will fire a spurious 'baby stalled' finding. The mismatch dates from the BAU-restoration text in the scheduled task description: 'BAU restored 2026-05-18 AM — 4:20 AM + 4:20 PM local, off-peak'.
findings.recommended_action: Trivial single-line edit to CLAUDE.md + a minor edit to tinker-pipeline-health.md. Either bundle with FND-02/FND-03 PROP next run, or operator self-edit. Low priority.
